title: Printers
description: Format typescript and graphic elements using computer software to produce publication-ready material. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of production and operating workers, such as inspectors, precision workers, machine setters and operators, assemblers, fabricators, and plant and system operators. Excludes team or work leaders. Format and proof text and images submitted by designers and clients into finished pages that can be printed. Includes digital and photo typesetting. May produce printing plates. (Based on 5 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Reprint originals for enlargement or in sections to be pieced together.
Operate special equipment to perform tasks such as transferring film to videotape or producing photographic enlargements.
Set or adjust machine controls according to specifications, operation type, or material requirements, and monitor equipment for malfunctions.
Clean or maintain photoprocessing or darkroom equipment using ultrasonic equipment or cleaning solutions, and perform minor repairs as needed.
Examine drawings, negatives, or photographic prints to determine changes required for retouching or restoration, and check prints for defects.
Produce timed prints with separate densities or color settings for each scene, and splicing broken or separated film as required.
Read work orders to determine required processes, techniques, materials, or equipment and examine job orders for specifications and quantities to be printed.
Produce color or black-and-white photographs, negatives, or slides by applying standard photographic reproduction techniques and procedures.
Review digital images for quality and edit graphics and photos using pixel or bitmap editing techniques and image retouching.
Load digital images onto computers directly from cameras or storage devices and convert various types of files for printing or for the Internet.
Select digital images for printing, specify the number of images, and direct the output to the printer using computer software.
Prepare sample layouts for approval and design print pages using desktop publishing software, positioning text and art elements in a visually appealing way.
Perform 'preflight' checks for required files, proofread text and images, and generate prepress proofs to approximate the final appearance.
Maintain, adjust, and clean equipment, and monitor inventory levels to order supplies as necessary.
Feed paper through press cylinders and load presses with the appropriate paper size, adjusting settings accordingly.
Adjust ink fountain flow rates, change press plates or cylinders, and operate and maintain laser plate-making equipment.
Monitor automated press operation systems and respond to fault, error, or alert messages to ensure quality production.
Confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities, and recommend or execute personnel actions such as training or evaluations.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, and collect and inspect random samples during print runs.
Plan and establish work schedules, assignments, and production sequences to meet production goals, also evaluating employee performance.
